author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: DeepSource ruleset alignment: tune .deepsource.toml so reported issues reflect repo policy (lands before all category triage)

Top rules by occurrence enumerated (authenticated dashboard/API) and dispositioned rule-by-rule: conflicts with repo idioms (TS-first patterns, docstring gate scope, intentional CLI process.exit etc.) get skipcq/toml suppressions WITH written rationale; genuinely useful rules stay enabled; post-alignment counts recorded on pm-vlnc; no blanket category disables without rationale

message: The 7.2k anti-pattern + 2.8k documentation stock strongly suggests default-JS-ruleset mismatch against a strict-TS codebase that already enforces 100% coverage, docstrings, jscpd, and 17-max ESLint suppressions. Aligning the analyzer beats 10k mechanical edits. DeepSource free tier supports rule disabling via .deepsource.toml and inline skipcq. This child BLOCKS meaningful counts for the category children — do it first.

value.note: Repository-side analyzer intent; currently ignored because DeepSource dashboard configuration is authoritative

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Live PR #565 evidence 2026-07-15: DeepSource dashboard payload, not .deepsource.toml, is authoritative. It reports dialect=typescript, environment=nodejs+vitest, style_guide=airbnb, ecma_version=2020, module_system=commonjs, cyclomatic threshold=low, documentation coverage 36.75%, and 1,937 JavaScript findings on the PR. The repo is ESM and its mandatory local/CI gate proves 100% module/export/member docstrings, so these dashboard settings conflict with repo policy. Repository TOML now states es-modules, but changing the external dashboard requires authenticated DeepSource access; this environment has neither CLI nor DSN/API credential. Do not blanket-disable source categories: first switch the dashboard to use repository TOML or align its settings per this item's acceptance criteria, then re-run and disposition remaining rules.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #565 final external-analyzer evidence: on head 4d3795d0 all 32 repository-owned/compatible checks are green, DeepSource Secrets and Shell pass, and only DeepSource JavaScript repeats the dashboard-owned legacy baseline. The repo-side ESM TOML and stronger exact 100% docstring/static/complexity gates are ready; authenticated dashboard alignment remains this open item's work and was intentionally not claimed in PR #565.

author: codex-audit-20260715

patch.value: Align authenticated DeepSource project settings and repository configuration with the ESM TypeScript codebase before treating the legacy dashboard backlog as actionable signal.
